name: Quinta do Pisão Loop
name_original: Quinta do Pisão — percurso circular no Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
description: Jog this 6.1-mile (9.8 km) moderate loop in Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, featuring mixed terrain, farm animals, and the Eira dos Fornicos Sc
region_intro: Jogging around Alcabideche offers diverse landscapes within the Lisboa region of Portugal. The area features varied terrain including dense forests and rolling hills, particularly within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Proximity to the Atlantic coast provides access to scenic coastal paths, while gentle hills offer varied inclines for runners. Best jogging routes around Alcabideche The most popular jogging route is Capuchos Trail (PR6 SNT), a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) difficult trail that takes about 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging experience through the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Rota do Litoral do Guincho — circular route PR4 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route provides coastal views within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Local runners also love the Rota das Aldeias — circular route PR3 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Jogging around Alcabideche is defined by dense forests, rolling hills, and coastal areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails. The routes in Alcabideche are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Alcabideche's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Alcabideche?
A: Alcabideche offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 45 routes documented on komoot. These routes cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging trails through diverse landscapes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on running trails around Alcabideche?
A: The region around Alcabideche boasts diverse terrain for runners. You can expect to find routes through the dense forests and rolling hills of the &lt;strong&gt;Sintra-Cascais Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, as well as scenic coastal paths near the Atlantic. There are also routes featuring gentle hills and elevated points, providing varied inclines for a good workout.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Alcabideche?
A: Yes, Alcabideche offers several running routes suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are at least two easy routes and over 20 moderate routes that can be enjoyed by those new to running or looking for a less strenuous experience. The &lt;strong&gt;Avenida Alcabideche&lt;/strong&gt; also features a dedicated, bike-friendly running track that is popular for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Alcabideche?
A: Alcabideche enjoys a mild climate, making it suitable for running year-round. However, spring (April to June) and fall (September to October) are particularly pleasant, offering ideal weather conditions and fewer tourists, which can enhance your running experience.

Q: Are there any dedicated running tracks or outdoor gyms in Alcabideche?
A: Yes, Alcabideche provides excellent facilities for runners. &lt;strong&gt;Avenida Alcabideche&lt;/strong&gt; features a dedicated, bike-friendly running track stretching over two kilometers, complete with various fitness equipment. The parallel &lt;strong&gt;Avenida República&lt;/strong&gt; offers over three kilometers of nearly straight running, and the area also has several calisthenics parks and outdoor gyms for supplementary training.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alcabideche?
A: Yes, Alcabideche features several popular circular running routes. Two notable options within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381031693/rota-do-litoral-do-guincho-circular-route-pr4-csc-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota do Litoral do Guincho — circular route PR4 CSC&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381029753/rota-das-aldeias-circular-route-pr3-csc-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota das Aldeias — circular route PR3 CSC&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through charming villages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Alcabideche?
A: Many natural parks and trails in Portugal, including those around Alcabideche, generally allow dogs, especially if kept on a leash. It's always a good idea to check specific park regulations or signage at trailheads, particularly within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while running in Alcabideche?
A: While running in Alcabideche, you can explore routes that offer glimpses of historical and natural beauty. Nearby attractions include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/205133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Moors&lt;/a&gt; and the enchanting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/185113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta da Regaleira&lt;/a&gt; in Sintra. For natural wonders, you might encounter the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/110232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca do Inferno (Hell’s Mouth) Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8001951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Alcabideche?
A: For families, the dedicated running track on &lt;strong&gt;Avenida Alcabideche&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice, as it's popular with people of all ages and offers a safe, flat surface. Additionally, some of the easier or moderate routes within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, particularly those with less elevation gain, can be suitable for families looking to enjoy nature together.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Alcabideche?
A: The running routes around Alcabideche are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners often praise the &lt;em&gt;varied landscapes&lt;/em&gt;, from dense forests to coastal views, and the &lt;em&gt;well-maintained trails&lt;/em&gt; within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The mix of challenging inclines and more relaxed paths also receives positive feedback.

Q: Are there any coastal running routes near Alcabideche?
A: Absolutely! Alcabideche's proximity to the Atlantic coast means you have easy access to stunning coastal running paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381114823/guincho-coastal-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guincho Coastal Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, offering incredible views of the sea and the Sintra mountains. The &lt;strong&gt;Ciclovia do Guincho&lt;/strong&gt;, a 17.4 km trail connecting Cascais Marina to Guincho Beach, is also a fantastic choice for sea-view runs.

Q: What is the difficulty level of running routes in Alcabideche?
A: The running routes in Alcabideche offer a good range of difficulty levels. You'll find a few easy routes, over 20 moderate routes, and approximately 23 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both casual joggers and experienced trail runners can find suitable challenges, from flat, paved paths to rugged trails with significant elevation changes.
tips: Quinta do Pisão is ideal for family walks and cycling for beginners, with specific areas for riding horses, donkeys, goats and sheep. Dogs must be on a leash and cycling is prohibited in some areas. There are alternative cycling and walking routes, avoiding the main roads and connecting to Serra de Sintra and Cascais/Estoril. Hikers can catch buses from Alcabideche to Cascais, Sintra or Estoril.

The Penhas do Marmeleiro Urban Park is located in Murches and extends over around four hectares. It has pedestrian and cycle paths, connecting Cascais to Serra de Sintra, integrating it into the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The intervention minimized natural slopes and revitalized an area previously affected by illegal deposits, using materials integrated into the landscape.

Eira dos Fornicos is an artistic installation in terracotta, a landscape landmark in Parque do Pisão. Its peculiar structure attracts attention. The point offers views of the Park and the surrounding nature. It is not uncommon to find horses and other animals running loose here.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Quinta do Pisão Loop?
faqs.answer: This moderate jogging route is approximately 9.77 km long and generally takes about 66 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The Quinta do Pisão Loop features a varied terrain. You'll encounter sections that are mostly paved, along with gravel paths, unpaved natural surfaces, and some street portions. There are also wooden platforms in certain areas to improve accessibility. Trail running shoes might be beneficial for the unpaved parts.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll jog through a mosaic of diverse vegetation and habitats within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Keep an eye out for farm animals like horses, donkeys, goats, and sheep, and you might even spot wild horses. The route also offers views of forest areas and the surrounding nature.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several interesting spots. You'll experience the natural beauty of Quinta do Pisão itself, and traverse parts of the Penhas do Marmeleiro Urban Park. You can also see the unique artistic installation, Eira dos Fornicos Sculpture.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Quinta do Pisão Loop integrates with the extensive network of trails within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Along this route, you'll also cover sections of official trails such as Rota das Quintas, Trilho das Vinhas, and Trilho do Marmeleiro.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to jog the Quinta do Pisão Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. During these seasons, the vegetation is vibrant, and the weather is generally mild. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is recommended, while winter can bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Quinta do Pisão Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Quinta do Pisão Loop is generally dog-friendly. However, it's important to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of farm animals and wildlife in the area, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the trails within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, including the Quinta do Pisão Loop. The park aims to be inclusive and accessible to all.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Quinta do Pisão Loop?
faqs.answer: Parking is available near the Quinta do Pisão visitor center. It's advisable to arrive earlier in the day, especially on weekends, to secure a spot as it can get busy.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the route is classified as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness' due to its length and elevation gain of about 130 meters, the overall Quinta do Pisão area also offers easier, shorter walks. If you're a beginner, you might consider starting with shorter sections or walking parts of this loop.

faqs.question: What amenities are available near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Quinta do Pisão area functions as an educational center and has some facilities. While there aren't specific cafes directly on the jogging loop, the visitor center area may offer basic amenities. The park also provides information in Braille along trails and transport for individuals with mobility problems at Casa de Cal.
